commands.appQuit.label: Quit Cmdr

@commands.appQuit.label.description: Command name for quitting the app. Appears in the macOS app menu and the keyboard-shortcuts list. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it.

commands.appHide.label: Hide Cmdr

@commands.appHide.label.description: Command name for hiding the app's windows. Appears in the macOS app menu and the keyboard-shortcuts list. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it.

commands.appHideOthers.label: Hide others

@commands.appHideOthers.label.description: Command name for hiding every OTHER app's windows (a standard macOS app-menu action). Appears in the macOS app menu and the keyboard-shortcuts list.

commands.appShowAll.label: Show all

@commands.appShowAll.label.description: Command name for un-hiding all apps' windows (a standard macOS app-menu action). Appears in the macOS app menu and the keyboard-shortcuts list.

commands.appAbout.label: About Cmdr

@commands.appAbout.label.description: Command name that opens the About window. Appears in the command palette and the macOS app menu. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it.

commands.appLicenseKey.seeDetails.label: See license details

@commands.appLicenseKey.seeDetails.label.description: Command name shown when the user already has a license: opens the license details. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.appLicenseKey.enterKey.label, which is shown instead when no license exists.

commands.appLicenseKey.enterKey.label: Enter license key

@commands.appLicenseKey.enterKey.label.description: Command name shown when the user has no license yet: opens the dialog to enter a license key. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.appLicenseKey.seeDetails.label, shown instead once a license exists.

commands.appCommandPalette.label: Open command palette

@commands.appCommandPalette.label.description: Command name that opens the command palette (the searchable list of all commands). "Command palette" is the established name of this UI; match the term used elsewhere in the app.

commands.appSettings.label: Open settings

@commands.appSettings.label.description: Command name that opens the Settings window. Appears in the command palette and the macOS app menu.

commands.appCheckForUpdates.label: Check for updates…

@commands.appCheckForUpdates.label.description: Command name that checks for a newer version of the app. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals it opens a flow rather than acting immediately; keep it.

commands.appCheckForUpdates.description: Check whether a newer version of Cmdr is available, and download it if so

@commands.appCheckForUpdates.description.description: Longer help text for the "Check for updates…" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it.

commands.cmdrOpenOnboarding.label: Onboarding…

@commands.cmdrOpenOnboarding.label.description: Command name that reopens the first-launch onboarding wizard. "Onboarding" refers to the guided first-time setup. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals it opens a wizard; keep it.

commands.cmdrOpenOnboarding.description: Reopen the onboarding wizard to review or change first-launch setup options

@commands.cmdrOpenOnboarding.description.description: Longer help text for the "Onboarding…" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.helpOpenShortcuts.label: Keyboard shortcuts

@commands.helpOpenShortcuts.label.description: Command name that opens a window listing every keyboard shortcut. Appears in the command palette and the Help menu.

commands.helpOpenShortcuts.description: Open a read-only window listing every keyboard shortcut, live-synced with your customizations

@commands.helpOpenShortcuts.description.description: Longer help text for the "Keyboard shortcuts" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.helpSendErrorReport.label: Send error report…

@commands.helpSendErrorReport.label.description: Command name that sends app logs to the team to help fix a problem. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals it opens a flow; keep it. "Error report" names the bundle of logs sent.

commands.helpSendErrorReport.description: Send Cmdr logs to the team to help fix something that went wrong

@commands.helpSendErrorReport.descriptio.description: Longer help text for the "Send error report…" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it.

commands.helpWhatsNew.label: What''s new

@commands.helpWhatsNew.label.description: Command name that shows what changed in recent releases. Appears in the command palette and the Help menu.

commands.helpWhatsNew.description: See what changed in the latest releases of Cmdr

@commands.helpWhatsNew.description.description: Longer help text for the "What's new" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it.

commands.feedbackSend.label: Send feedback

@commands.feedbackSend.label.description: Command name that opens a form to send feedback to the app's maker. Appears in the command palette and the Help menu.

commands.feedbackSend.description: Tell the maker of Cmdr what you think: ideas, wishes, anything

@commands.feedbackSend.description.description: Longer help text for the "Send feedback" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cmdr" is the brand name, do not translate it. The tone is warm and inviting.

commands.searchOpen.label: Search files

@commands.searchOpen.label.description: Command name that opens the file search. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navGoToPath.label: Go to path…

@commands.navGoToPath.label.description: Command name that opens a dialog to jump to a typed path. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals it opens a dialog; keep it. "Path" means a filesystem location like ~/Documents.

commands.navGoToPath.description: Jump the focused pane to a typed, pasted, or recent path.

@commands.navGoToPath.description.description: Longer help text for the "Go to path…" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Pane" is one of the app's two side-by-side file lists.

commands.favoritesAdd.label: Add to favorites

@commands.favoritesAdd.label.description: Command name that adds the current folder to the Favorites list. Appears in the command palette. "Favorites" is the named list of bookmarked folders; match the term used in the volume switcher UI.

commands.favoritesAdd.description: Add the focused pane''s current folder to the switcher''s Favorites.

@commands.favoritesAdd.description.description: Longer help text for the "Add to favorites" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Pane" is one of the two side-by-side file lists; "switcher" is the volume/location switcher; "Favorites" is its bookmarked-folders list.

commands.downloadsGoToLatest.label: Go to latest download

@commands.downloadsGoToLatest.label.description: Command name that opens the Downloads folder with the newest file selected. Appears in the command palette.

commands.downloadsGoToLatest.description: Open ~/Downloads and select the most recent file.

@commands.downloadsGoToLatest.descriptio.description: Longer help text for the "Go to latest download" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "~/Downloads" is a filesystem path; keep it literally.

commands.viewShowHidden.label: Toggle hidden files

@commands.viewShowHidden.label.description: Command name that shows or hides hidden files (those normally not displayed, like dotfiles). Appears in the command palette and the View menu.

commands.viewBriefMode.label: Switch to Brief view

@commands.viewBriefMode.label.description: Command name that switches the file list to Brief view (a compact, multi-column layout). "Brief" is the view's proper name; match the term used in the View menu.

commands.viewFullMode.label: Switch to Full view

@commands.viewFullMode.label.description: Command name that switches the file list to Full view (a detailed single-column layout with columns). "Full" is the view's proper name; match the term used in the View menu.

commands.viewSetMode.label: Set pane view mode

@commands.viewSetMode.label.description: Internal command name for setting a specific pane's view mode, shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two side-by-side file lists.

commands.viewZoomSet75.label: Zoom to 75%

@commands.viewZoomSet75.label.description: Command name that sets the UI text size to 75%. Appears in the command palette. Keep "75%" as-is.

commands.viewZoomSet100.label: Zoom to 100%

@commands.viewZoomSet100.label.description: Command name that sets the UI text size to 100% (the default). Appears in the command palette. Keep "100%" as-is.

commands.viewZoomSet125.label: Zoom to 125%

@commands.viewZoomSet125.label.description: Command name that sets the UI text size to 125%. Appears in the command palette. Keep "125%" as-is.

commands.viewZoomSet150.label: Zoom to 150%

@commands.viewZoomSet150.label.description: Command name that sets the UI text size to 150%. Appears in the command palette. Keep "150%" as-is.

commands.viewZoomIn.label: Zoom in

@commands.viewZoomIn.label.description: Command name that increases the UI text size by one step. Appears in the command palette.

commands.viewZoomOut.label: Zoom out

@commands.viewZoomOut.label.description: Command name that decreases the UI text size by one step. Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortByName.label: Sort by name

@commands.sortByName.label.description: Command name that sorts the file list by file name. Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortByExtension.label: Sort by extension

@commands.sortByExtension.label.description: Command name that sorts the file list by file extension (the part after the last dot, like .txt). Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortByModified.label: Sort by date modified

@commands.sortByModified.label.description: Command name that sorts the file list by last-modified date. Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortBySize.label: Sort by size

@commands.sortBySize.label.description: Command name that sorts the file list by file size. Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortByCreated.label: Sort by date created

@commands.sortByCreated.label.description: Command name that sorts the file list by creation date. Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortAscending.label: Sort ascending

@commands.sortAscending.label.description: Command name that sets the sort order to ascending (A to Z, smallest first). Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortDescending.label: Sort descending

@commands.sortDescending.label.description: Command name that sets the sort order to descending (Z to A, largest first). Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortToggleOrder.label: Toggle sort order

@commands.sortToggleOrder.label.description: Command name that flips between ascending and descending sort. Appears in the command palette.

commands.sortSet.label: Set pane sort

@commands.sortSet.label.description: Internal command name for setting a specific pane's sort, shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two side-by-side file lists.

commands.paneSwitch.label: Switch pane

@commands.paneSwitch.label.description: Command name that moves focus to the other pane. "Pane" is one of the two side-by-side file lists. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paneSwap.label: Swap panes

@commands.paneSwap.label.description: Command name that swaps the contents of the left and right panes. "Panes" are the two side-by-side file lists. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paneLeftVolumeChooser.label: Open left volume chooser

@commands.paneLeftVolumeChooser.label.description: Command name that opens the volume/location chooser for the LEFT pane. "Volume" is a disk or mounted location. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paneRightVolumeChooser.label: Open right volume chooser

@commands.paneRightVolumeChooser.label.description: Command name that opens the volume/location chooser for the RIGHT pane. "Volume" is a disk or mounted location. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paneCopyPathLeftToRight.label: Copy path from left to right pane

@commands.paneCopyPathLeftToRight.label.description: Command name that opens the left pane's location in the right pane. "Path" is the folder location; "pane" is one of the two file lists. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paneCopyPathLeftToRight.descrip: Open the left pane’s location on the right. When the left pane is focused and the cursor is on a folder, that folder opens on the right instead.

@commands.paneCopyPathLeftToRight.descri.description: Longer help text for the "Copy path from left to right pane" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cursor" is the highlighted row in the file list.

commands.paneCopyPathRightToLeft.label: Copy path from right to left pane

@commands.paneCopyPathRightToLeft.label.description: Command name that opens the right pane's location in the left pane. "Path" is the folder location; "pane" is one of the two file lists. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paneCopyPathRightToLeft.descrip: Open the right pane’s location on the left. When the right pane is focused and the cursor is on a folder, that folder opens on the left instead.

@commands.paneCopyPathRightToLeft.descri.description: Longer help text for the "Copy path from right to left pane" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cursor" is the highlighted row in the file list.

commands.paneRefresh.label: Refresh pane

@commands.paneRefresh.label.description: Internal command name for re-reading the focused pane's folder, shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two side-by-side file lists.

commands.tabNew.label: New tab

@commands.tabNew.label.description: Command name that opens a new tab in the focused pane. Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabClose.label: Close tab

@commands.tabClose.label.description: Command name that closes the current tab. Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabReopen.label: Reopen closed tab

@commands.tabReopen.label.description: Command name that reopens the most recently closed tab. Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabNext.label: Next tab

@commands.tabNext.label.description: Command name that moves to the next tab. Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabPrev.label: Previous tab

@commands.tabPrev.label.description: Command name that moves to the previous tab. Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabTogglePin.label: Toggle pin tab

@commands.tabTogglePin.label.description: Command name that pins or unpins the current tab (a pinned tab stays open). Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabCloseOthers.label: Close other tabs

@commands.tabCloseOthers.label.description: Command name that closes every tab except the current one. Appears in the command palette.

commands.tabMcpAction.label: Pane tab action

@commands.tabMcpAction.label.description: Internal command name for a tab action driven by the automation interface, shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two side-by-side file lists.

commands.navUp.label: Select previous file

@commands.navUp.label.description: Command name for moving the cursor up one row in the file list. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.navDown.label: Select next file

@commands.navDown.label.description: Command name for moving the cursor down one row in the file list. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.navOpen.label: Open file or folder

@commands.navOpen.label.description: Command name that opens the file or folder under the cursor. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navParent.label: Go to parent folder

@commands.navParent.label.description: Command name that navigates up to the parent folder. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navHome.label: Go to first file

@commands.navHome.label.description: Command name that moves the cursor to the first item in the file list. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navEnd.label: Go to last file

@commands.navEnd.label.description: Command name that moves the cursor to the last item in the file list. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navPageUp.label: Page up

@commands.navPageUp.label.description: Command name that scrolls the file list up by one page. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navPageDown.label: Page down

@commands.navPageDown.label.description: Command name that scrolls the file list down by one page. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navBack.label: Go back

@commands.navBack.label.description: Command name that navigates back to the previous location in history. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navForward.label: Go forward

@commands.navForward.label.description: Command name that navigates forward in history. Appears in the command palette.

commands.navOpenUnderCursor.label: Open item under cursor

@commands.navOpenUnderCursor.label.description: Internal command name for opening the item under the cursor, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.cursorMoveTo.label: Move pane cursor

@commands.cursorMoveTo.label.description: Internal command name for moving the cursor to a position, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two file lists.

commands.cursorScrollTo.label: Scroll pane to index

@commands.cursorScrollTo.label.description: Internal command name for scrolling a pane to a given row, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two file lists.

commands.navLeft.label: Move to left column

@commands.navLeft.label.description: Command name for moving the cursor to the column on the left in Brief view (the multi-column layout). Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.navRight.label: Move to right column

@commands.navRight.label.description: Command name for moving the cursor to the column on the right in Brief view (the multi-column layout). Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.navFirstInFull.label: Jump to first file

@commands.navFirstInFull.label.description: Command name for jumping to the first item in Full view (the detailed single-column layout). Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.navLastInFull.label: Jump to last file

@commands.navLastInFull.label.description: Command name for jumping to the last item in Full view (the detailed single-column layout). Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.fileRename.label: Rename

@commands.fileRename.label.description: Command name that renames the selected file or folder. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label.

commands.fileView.label: View

@commands.fileView.label.description: Command name that opens the built-in viewer for the selected file. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label.

commands.fileEdit.label: Edit in default editor

@commands.fileEdit.label.description: Command name that opens the selected file in the user's default editor app. Appears in the command palette.

commands.fileCopy.label: Copy

@commands.fileCopy.label.description: Command name for the F5 copy-to-other-pane operation. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label. Distinct from "Copy to clipboard".

commands.fileMove.label: Move

@commands.fileMove.label.description: Command name for the F6 move-to-other-pane operation. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label. Distinct from "Cut to clipboard".

commands.editCopy.label: Copy to clipboard

@commands.editCopy.label.description: Command name that copies the selected files to the system clipboard (for pasting). Appears in the command palette. Distinct from the F5 "Copy" operation.

commands.editCopy.description: Copy selected files to clipboard for pasting

@commands.editCopy.description.description: Longer help text for the "Copy to clipboard" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.editCut.label: Cut to clipboard

@commands.editCut.label.description: Command name that cuts the selected files to the system clipboard (pasting will move them). Appears in the command palette.

commands.editCut.description: Cut selected files (paste will move them)

@commands.editCut.description.description: Longer help text for the "Cut to clipboard" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.editPaste.label: Paste

@commands.editPaste.label.description: Command name that pastes clipboard files into the current folder. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label.

commands.editPaste.description: Paste files from clipboard into current folder

@commands.editPaste.description.description: Longer help text for the "Paste" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.editPasteAsMove.label: Move here

@commands.editPasteAsMove.label.description: Command name that pastes clipboard files as a move (rather than a copy). Appears in the command palette.

commands.editPasteAsMove.description: Paste files from clipboard as a move

@commands.editPasteAsMove.description.description: Longer help text for the "Move here" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.fileNewFolder.label: New folder

@commands.fileNewFolder.label.description: Command name that creates a new folder. Appears in the command palette.

commands.fileNewFile.label: Create new file

@commands.fileNewFile.label.description: Command name that creates a new empty file. Appears in the command palette.

commands.fileDelete.label: Delete

@commands.fileDelete.label.description: Command name that deletes the selected files (to the system trash). Appears in the command palette. A terse control label.

commands.fileDeletePermanently.label: Delete permanently

@commands.fileDeletePermanently.label.description: Command name that deletes the selected files permanently, bypassing the trash. Appears in the command palette. The word "permanently" carries the warning that this can't be undone.

commands.dialogConfirm.label: Confirm open dialog

@commands.dialogConfirm.label.description: Internal command name for confirming an open dialog, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.fileShowInFinder.mac.label: Show in Finder

@commands.fileShowInFinder.mac.label.description: Command name (macOS) that reveals the selected file in Finder, the macOS file manager. "Finder" is the macOS app name; do not translate it. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.fileShowInFinder.other.label for other platforms.

commands.fileShowInFinder.other.label: Show in file manager

@commands.fileShowInFinder.other.label.description: Command name (non-macOS) that reveals the selected file in the system file manager. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.fileShowInFinder.mac.label, used on macOS.

commands.fileCopyPath.label: Copy path to clipboard

@commands.fileCopyPath.label.description: Command name that copies the selected file's full path to the clipboard. "Path" is the file's filesystem location. Appears in the command palette.

@commands.fileCopyCurrentDirectoryPath.l.description: Command name that copies the current folder's full path to the clipboard. "Directory" and "path" are filesystem terms. Appears in the command palette.

commands.fileCopyFilename.label: Copy filename

@commands.fileCopyFilename.label.description: Command name that copies the selected file's name (without the folder path) to the clipboard. Appears in the command palette.

commands.fileGetInfo.mac.label: Get info

@commands.fileGetInfo.mac.label.description: Command name (macOS) that opens the file's info panel. "Get info" matches the macOS Finder term; reuse the localized macOS wording for that menu item. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.fileGetInfo.other.label for other platforms.

commands.fileGetInfo.other.label: File properties

@commands.fileGetInfo.other.label.description: Command name (non-macOS) that opens the file's properties panel. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.fileGetInfo.mac.label, used on macOS.

commands.fileQuickLook.mac.label: Quick look

@commands.fileQuickLook.mac.label.description: Command name (macOS) that previews the selected file with Quick Look. "Quick Look" is the macOS feature name; reuse the localized macOS wording. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.fileQuickLook.other.label for other platforms.

commands.fileQuickLook.other.label: Preview

@commands.fileQuickLook.other.label.description: Command name (non-macOS) that previews the selected file. Appears in the command palette. Paired with commands.fileQuickLook.mac.label, used on macOS.

commands.fileContextMenu.label: Open context menu

@commands.fileContextMenu.label.description: Command name that opens the right-click context menu for the file under the cursor. Appears in the command palette.

commands.fileContextMenu.description: Opens the context menu for the file under the cursor

@commands.fileContextMenu.description.description: Longer help text for the "Open context menu" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Cursor" is the highlighted row in the file list.

commands.cloudMakeOffline.label: Make available offline

@commands.cloudMakeOffline.label.description: Command name that downloads a cloud-stored file so it stays available without internet. Appears in the command palette.

commands.cloudMakeOffline.description: Downloads a cloud-stored file so it stays available without an internet connection

@commands.cloudMakeOffline.description.description: Longer help text for the "Make available offline" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.cloudRemoveDownload.label: Remove download

@commands.cloudRemoveDownload.label.description: Command name that removes the local copy of a cloud file, leaving it online-only. Appears in the command palette.

commands.cloudRemoveDownload.description: Removes the local copy of a cloud file, leaving it available online only

@commands.cloudRemoveDownload.descriptio.description: Longer help text for the "Remove download" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette.

commands.selectionToggle.label: Toggle selection

@commands.selectionToggle.label.description: Command name that selects or deselects the file under the cursor. Appears in the command palette.

commands.selectionToggleAndDown.label: Toggle selection and move down

@commands.selectionToggleAndDown.label.description: Command name that selects/deselects the file under the cursor, then moves down one row. Appears in the command palette.

commands.selectionToggleAndDown.descript: Selects or deselects the file under the cursor, then moves down (Total Commander style)

@commands.selectionToggleAndDown.descrip.description: Longer help text for the "Toggle selection and move down" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Total Commander" is a well-known file manager app name; do not translate it.

commands.selectionSelectAll.label: Select all

@commands.selectionSelectAll.label.description: Command name that selects every file in the list. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label.

commands.selectionDeselectAll.label: Deselect all

@commands.selectionDeselectAll.label.description: Command name that clears the selection. Appears in the command palette. A terse control label.

commands.selectionSelectFiles.label: Select files…

@commands.selectionSelectFiles.label.description: Command name that opens a dialog to select files matching a pattern. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals it opens a dialog; keep it. Appears in the command palette.

commands.selectionSelectFiles.descriptio: Opens the Select files dialog to add matching files to the selection

@commands.selectionSelectFiles.descripti.description: Longer help text for the "Select files…" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Select files" names the dialog it opens.

commands.selectionDeselectFiles.label: Deselect files…

@commands.selectionDeselectFiles.label.description: Command name that opens a dialog to deselect files matching a pattern. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals it opens a dialog; keep it. Appears in the command palette.

commands.selectionDeselectFiles.descript: Opens the Deselect files dialog to remove matching files from the selection

@commands.selectionDeselectFiles.descrip.description: Longer help text for the "Deselect files…" command, shown as a secondary line in the command palette. "Deselect files" names the dialog it opens.

commands.selectionMcpSelect.label: Select range in pane

@commands.selectionMcpSelect.label.description: Internal command name for selecting a range of files, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two file lists.

commands.selectionMcpSelectByNames.label: Select files by name in pane

@commands.selectionMcpSelectByNames.labe.description: Internal command name for selecting files by name, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two file lists.

commands.networkSelectHost.label: Select network host

@commands.networkSelectHost.label.description: Command name that opens the selected network host (server) in the network browser. "Host" is a server on the network. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.networkRefresh.label: Refresh network hosts

@commands.networkRefresh.label.description: Command name that re-scans the network for hosts (servers). Appears in the command palette.

commands.shareBack.label: Back to host list

@commands.shareBack.label.description: Command name that goes back from a server's shares to the list of network hosts (servers). Appears in the command palette.

commands.shareSelectShare.label: Connect to share

@commands.shareSelectShare.label.description: Command name that connects to the selected network share (a shared folder on a server). Appears in the command palette.

commands.volumeSelect.label: Select volume

@commands.volumeSelect.label.description: Command name that opens the selected volume (disk or mounted location) in the volume chooser. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.volumeClose.label: Close volume chooser

@commands.volumeClose.label.description: Command name that closes the volume chooser. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette).

commands.volumeSelectByName.label: Select pane volume by name

@commands.volumeSelectByName.label.description: Internal command name for selecting a pane's volume by name, driven by the automation interface; shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette). "Pane" is one of the two file lists; "volume" is a disk or mounted location.

commands.aboutOpenWebsite.label: Open website

@commands.aboutOpenWebsite.label.description: Command name (in the About window) that opens the app's website in a browser. Appears in the command palette.

commands.aboutOpenUpgrade.label: Open upgrade page

@commands.aboutOpenUpgrade.label.description: Command name (in the About window) that opens the upgrade/purchase page in a browser. Appears in the command palette.

commands.aboutClose.label: Close About window

@commands.aboutClose.label.description: Command name that closes the About window. Appears in the command palette.

commands.paletteUp.label: Previous result

@commands.paletteUp.label.description: Command name for moving up one row in the command palette's result list. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette itself).

commands.paletteDown.label: Next result

@commands.paletteDown.label.description: Command name for moving down one row in the command palette's result list. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette itself).

commands.paletteExecute.label: Execute command

@commands.paletteExecute.label.description: Command name for running the highlighted command in the command palette. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette itself).

commands.paletteClose.label: Close palette

@commands.paletteClose.label.description: Command name for closing the command palette. Shown only in the keyboard-shortcuts list (not the palette itself).

@commands.handler.favoriteAdded.description: Success toast after adding the current folder to favorites (via the command palette, menu, or shortcut). The folder name is shown in straight double quotes.

placeholders.name: the folder's name (its last path segment), e.g. "Documents"

commands.handler.favoriteAddFailed: Couldn't add that folder to favorites. Try again?

@commands.handler.favoriteAddFailed.description: Error toast shown when adding a folder to favorites didn't go through. Friendly and reassuring; ends with an offer to retry. Uses a contraction ("Couldn't").

@commands.handler.tabLimitReached.description: Warning toast shown when the user tries to open or reopen a tab but the per-pane tab limit is already reached. Terse. Shown for both new-tab and reopen-tab attempts.

commands.handler.noRecentlyClosedTabs: No recently closed tabs in this pane.

@commands.handler.noRecentlyClosedTabs.description: Warning toast shown when the user tries to reopen a recently closed tab but the pane has no closed-tab history.

@commands.handler.cloudDownloadFailed.description: Error toast when making a cloud file available offline (downloading it) didn't go through. Uses a contraction ("Couldn't").

placeholders.detail: the raw underlying error text appended after the sentence (a technical message, often in English)

@commands.handler.cloudRemoveDownloadFai.description: Error toast when removing a cloud file's local download (freeing space) didn't go through. Uses a contraction ("Couldn't").

placeholders.detail: the raw underlying error text appended after the sentence (a technical message, often in English)

commands.handler.zoomResetHintShortcut: You can reset the zoom level to 100% by {shortcut}.

@commands.handler.zoomResetHintShortcut.description: Trailing hint appended to the zoom-changed toast, telling the user how to reset zoom when a reset shortcut is bound. "100%" is literal.

placeholders.shortcut: the keyboard shortcut that resets zoom to 100%, e.g. "⌘0"; do NOT translate the glyphs or key names

commands.handler.zoomResetHintMenu: You can reset the zoom level to 100% at View > Zoom > 100%.

@commands.handler.zoomResetHintMenu.description: Trailing hint appended to the zoom-changed toast when no reset shortcut is bound, pointing at the menu path instead. "View > Zoom > 100%" is a literal menu path; "View" and "Zoom" are menu names that match the app's menu bar; "100%" is literal.

@commands.handler.zoomReset.description: Info toast shown when the user sets the zoom level back to 100%. "100%" is literal.

@commands.handler.zoomIncreased.description: Info toast shown when the user increases the zoom level. Ends with a reset hint.

placeholders.size: the new zoom percentage as a number, e.g. "125" (the % sign follows it in the template)
placeholders.hint: a pre-built sentence telling the user how to reset zoom to 100% (already translated separately)

@commands.handler.zoomDecreased.description: Info toast shown when the user decreases the zoom level. Ends with a reset hint.

placeholders.size: the new zoom percentage as a number, e.g. "90" (the % sign follows it in the template)
placeholders.hint: a pre-built sentence telling the user how to reset zoom to 100% (already translated separately)
